act: Wellness and Oversight for Psychological Resources Act

il_225_155_unlicensed_public_therapy.description: Illinois prohibits non-licensed-professional therapy or psychotherapy services offered to the public

il_225_155_recorded_supplementary_suppor.description: A licensed professional uses AI for supplementary support while the therapy session is recorded or transcribed

il_225_155_independent_therapeutic_decis.description: A licensed professional allows AI to make independent therapeutic decisions

il_225_155_therapeutic_communication.description: A licensed professional allows AI to directly interact with clients in therapeutic communication

il_225_155_unreviewed_recommendations.description: A licensed professional allows AI to generate therapeutic recommendations or treatment plans without licensed review and approval

il_225_155_emotion_detection.description: A licensed professional allows AI to detect emotions or mental states

il_225_155_confidentiality.description: Records kept by a licensed professional and therapy-related communications exist

obligations.content: Provide the Section 15 disclosure in writing.
Inform the patient or the patient's legally authorized representative in writing that artificial intelligence will be used.
Inform the patient or the patient's legally authorized representative in writing of the specific purpose of the artificial intelligence tool or system that will be used.
Provide the written disclosure to the patient or the patient's legally authorized representative and obtain consent from that person.

Obtain clear, explicit, informed, voluntary, specific, written consent, including consent provided by electronic means, before using artificial intelligence for recorded or transcribed supplementary support.
Consent for this use of artificial intelligence must remain revocable by the individual.
Do not rely on general or broad terms of use as the required consent.
Hovering, muting, pausing, or closing digital content is not valid consent.
Consent obtained through deceptive actions is invalid.

Treat records kept by the licensed professional and communications with an individual seeking therapy or psychotherapy services as confidential, and do not disclose them except as required under the Mental Health and Developmental Disabilities Confidentiality Act.

prohibitions.text: Unlicensed individuals, corporations, entities, and unregulated artificial intelligence systems cannot provide, advertise, or otherwise offer therapy or psychotherapy services to the public in Illinois.
A licensed professional may not allow artificial intelligence to make independent therapeutic decisions.
A licensed professional may not allow artificial intelligence to directly interact with clients in any form of therapeutic communication.
A licensed professional may not allow artificial intelligence to generate therapeutic recommendations or treatment plans without review and approval by the licensed professional.
A licensed professional may not allow artificial intelligence to detect emotions or mental states.

notes: The Act is gated on therapy or psychotherapy services and does not trigger from mental-health domain tagging alone.
Exempt religious counseling, peer support, and public self-help or educational material block the Act entirely.
